full_prompt: Design a monolithic 5083-H111 aluminum base-plate chassis deck for a collegiate autonomous robot contest benchmark on a 3000 mm x 2000 mm playfield, respecting the benchmark perimeter rule: start-size silhouette fits a 1200 mm perimeter (e.g., 300 x 300 mm footprint) and the expanded-size silhouette fits a 1400 mm perimeter (e.g., 400 x 300 mm). The deck is a single flat plate (no welded stiffeners; all pockets and rib features machined from a single billet) with a 300 mm x 300 mm footprint and 8 mm thickness, with an extended wing on one side within the expanded-size envelope (a hinged secondary piece is NOT part of this CAD prompt; only the base deck is to be designed). Geometry: 300 mm x 300 mm square base, 8 mm plate thickness, four pocketed weight-reduction cavities 80 mm x 80 mm x 4 mm deep on a 150 mm spacing, twelve M5 through-holes (5.5 mm clearance) in a 3x4 grid on 60 mm centers for motor + electronics mounting, four 12 mm through-bores at the corners for vertical M12 standoffs, two 25 mm D-shaped cutouts on opposite edges for drive-wheel clearance, integral front edge recess 200 mm wide x 10 mm deep for a pick-up manipulator, total bounding envelope 300 mm x 300 mm x 8 mm. Material 5083-H111 aluminum with E 71 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2660 kg/m^3, yield 145 MPa, UTS 310 MPa. Payload carried: 3.5 kg of electronics + batteries + actuators distributed across the 3x4 grid, plus a 2.0 kg grip end-effector at the front edge recess producing a 0.3 m moment arm from the chassis center. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (match-play operating): the robot carries 3.5 kg on-deck plus 2.0 kg at the front edge recess and accelerates at 1.5 m/s^2 while on a 4 mm play-mat surface; treat as a 1g vertical + 0.15g horizontal body-force load with the four corner standoff bores fully fixed. Load Case 2 (opponent collision): a 50 N horizontal impact on the front edge recess lasting 100 ms per benchmark General Rules collision-survivability practice; evaluate as 50 N quasi-static on the front edge. Load Case 3 (lift-and-drop during setup): 5g vertical downward drop (representing a 0.25 m accidental drop) with only the four corner standoff bores fixed, evaluating the plate as a cantilevered disk. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 operating, peak von Mises in the deck must not exceed 72 MPa (yield/2.0, standard robotics engineering practice); (R2) under LC2 collision, peak stress 97 MPa (yield/1.5, transient); (R3) under LC3 drop, peak stress 97 MPa AND maximum deflection of any point on the deck must not exceed 3.0 mm to preserve motor gearhead alignment; (R4) first six natural frequencies must exceed 60 Hz to avoid coupling with the 20-50 Hz typical drive-motor harmonics of brushed DC motors at 3000-6000 rpm on a typical benchmark drivetrain; (R5) deck mass must not exceed 1.6 kg to leave ~5 kg budget for electronics and manipulator in the benchmark total mass limit; (R6) the deck silhouette must fit inside the 1200 mm start-perimeter (sum of perimeter edges <= 1200 mm, trivially 4 x 300 = 1200 mm exactly at limit); the expanded wing footprint including any deck extension must not exceed 1400 mm perimeter. Thermal, fluid, buckling, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 5083-H111 aluminum base-plate chassis deck for a collegiate autonomous robot contest on a 3000 x 2000 mm playfield. The deck is a single billet-machined 300 x 300 x 8 mm flat plate with integral weight-reduction pockets, a 3x4 grid of M5 motor/electronics mounting holes, four corner M12 standoff bores, D-shaped drive-wheel clearance cutouts, and a front-edge recess for a pick-up manipulator. The benchmark perimeter rule limits the start silhouette to a 1200 mm perimeter and the expanded silhouette to 1400 mm. Use yield/2.0 operating and yield/1.5 transient safety factors per standard robotics engineering practice.

Envelope: 300 x 300 x 8 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 match-play operating: 3.5 kg distributed on the 3x4 grid plus 2.0 kg at the front-edge recess (0.3 m moment arm), 1 g vertical + 0.15 g horizontal body force (1.5 m/s^2 accel), four corner standoffs fixed.
- LC2 opponent collision: 50 N quasi-static horizontal at the front-edge recess (100 ms impact).
- LC3 lift-and-drop: 5 g vertical down (0.25 m accidental drop), only the four corner standoff bores fixed.

prompt.title: Collegiate autonomous robot chassis deck plate benchmark
prompt.brief: 5083-H111 flat base plate 300x300x8 with integral wheel cutouts + motor grid + front edge recess; 1200/1400 mm perimeter.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Plate is too thick to be buckling-critical at these loads.
